mood: a playable archive unearthed in section, half ruin and half reference machine
material_language: cut stone slabs with beveled section faces
aged parchment concept-paper embedded in strata
painted thumbnail panels with rough fantasy-art edges
lighting: warm bronze grazing light across cut edges, cold blue-violet glints inside apertures, and a hazy pale backlight that keeps the strata readable
object_inventory: one broad horizontal architectural cutaway with three to four terrain-like strata
two hero-scale exposed chambers carrying the main source fields
disciplined clusters of small painted archive fragments absorbed into stone and vellum
bronze conduit paths linking agentic and game-dev layers without becoming a signal board
foggy negative-space bands separating hoverable cuts and apertures
negative_constraints: no readable words, labels, filenames, logos, handles, alphanumeric callouts, or UI chrome inside the image
no desk, browser dashboard, SaaS landing page, card grid, cabinet, shelf wall, or generic software interface
do not copy the reference subject, armor, animal, pose, logo, or page chrome literally
do not use source-summary cards; every anchor must be a physical mark, aperture, edge, surface, or material interruption
avoid recent visual worlds: ceramic aperture atlas, torn bouquet, black tape scan field, cloth taxonomy, chrome observatory, nocturne current field
no equal-weight grid of source objects
no literal depiction of the source reference image
